label: origin_median
definition: Context feature (not a KPI)

label: Other (Defensive Threat)
definition: The change in the threat of conceding by other actions. This KPI consists of 'PXT_FOUL' and 'PXT_NO_VIDEO' and is attributed to all players of the opponent team.

label: Bypassed Opponents Number
definition: Number of actions with which opponents could be bypassed (dribble, pass, cross, set piece).

label: n_matches_loaded
definition: Context feature (not a KPI)

label: Number of Fouls Won
definition: Number of Fouls Won measures how often a player is fouled by an opponent during the match, based on tagging or referee decisions.

label: Passes (Opponent Threat)
definition: Changing the opponent's goal threat through your own passing game. Progressive passing reduces the opponent's goal threat, while regressive passing and mispassing (usually) increases the opponent's goal threat.

label: Blocks (Opponent Threat)
definition: The change in the opponent's goal threat due to blocked passes, shots and set-pieces. The change is calculated from the difference between the opponent's goal threat after the block and the opponent's goal threat before the block.

label: age
definition: Context feature (not a KPI)

label: Regressive dribbles
definition: Sub-KPI of PXT_DRIBBLE. The negative change in one´s own threat of scoring through dribbling without losing the ball. Every dribble that was successful and lowers the goal threat counts towards this.

label: Packing non-shot-based xG
definition: Packing xG (pxG) include not only include shots on goal but also all potential shooting positions, even if no shot on goal is taken. In contrast to the shot-based xG, the packing xG goes one step further by attributing a value to these positions & does not rely on a shot being taken. A team is there

label: Shot-based xG
definition: Shot-based expected goals (xG) is a figure that adds a value to all shot attempts. This includes shots from open-play, direct free kicks and penalty kicks. In order to generate a figure that values probability of a goal being scored from a shot, parameters such as shot distance, shot angle, pressure

label: Second Ball Wins
definition: The win of the 'second ball' phase is achieved if a player manages to bring enough control over the ball (pressure <40).

label: Distance Covered By Dribbles
definition: Difference in the distance to the opposing goal due to the dribble (between release of the ball and reception).

label: Progressive dribbles
definition: Sub-KPI of PXT_DRIBBLE. The positive change in one´s goal-goal threat through dribbling. Every dribbling that increases your goal-goal threat counts here.

label: Play Duration
definition: Match duration in seconds. Every second of the match is listed here (including injury time).

label: Regressive set pieces
definition: Sub-KPI of PXT_SETPIECE. The negative change of a team’s goal threat through set-pieces to the teammate. Every set-piece that lowers the goal threat and ends with the teammate is counted here.

label: Blocks
definition: The change in a team’s goal threat due to blocked passes, shots and set-pieces. The change is calculated from the difference between the goal threat after the block and the goal threat before the block.

label: Progressive passes
definition: Sub-KPI of PXT_PASS. The positive change in a team’s goal threat through passing. Every pass that increases a team’s goal threat counts here. This also includes passes that end with the opponent if a team’s goal threat is higher after the pass than before the pass.

label: Post-Shot xG
definition: Post-shot xG is a value of all shots on target (all shots on goal that go in or are saved by the goalkeeper). In addition to the shot-based xG variables (shot distance, shot angle, pressure on the shooter, opponents between ball and goal, phase of play, and type of shot), the "likely finishing posit

label: Shot-Creating Actions
definition: Shot-Creating Actions are the two offensive actions directly leading to a shot attempt. This includes passes, and fouls drawn that set up a teammate’s shot. Specifically, an Shot-Creating Actions consists of the last two events before a shot, regardless of whether they occur in open play or from set

label: Second Ball Starts
definition: The action that results in a 'second ball' phase where a high amount of pressure on the ball carrying player is applied and where no team has real control over the ball. It is typically the result of a high pass into an aerial duel, a blocked pass/shot or a saved pass or shot.

label: Offensive Touches
definition: Any touch on the ball where a teammate has previously been on the ball is called an offensive touch.

label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving
definition: The sum of all opponents who are bypassed from a pass, from the perspective of the receiver.

label: Fouled
definition: The change in a team's goal threat which is achieved by being fouled.

label: Bypassed Opponents Without Verification
definition: Bypassed opponents without the pass being ‘verified' by the pass receiver. If a player is passed to in a high pressure situation, the bypassed opponents from this pass are not fully counted until the point that the ball is completely under control. As the value here does not include verification by

label: Opponent set pieces (Defensive Threat)
definition: The change in the threat of conceding through set pieces from the opponent (progressive set pieces and set regressive pieces). The change in the threat of conceding is distributed to the responsible players based on the responsability model. The responsible players are rewarded for regressive set pi

label: Failed set pieces
definition: Sub-KPI of PXT_PASS. The negative change in goal threat due to set-pieces to the opponent or offside. Any set-piece that lowers the goal threat and ends at the opponent/offside is included here.

label: Expected Shot Assists
definition: A model-based estimate of how many shot assists a player is expected to produce from their passes or set pieces. For each pass/set piece, the model assigns a probability that it will directly become the final action before a teammate’s shot (per our event-linking rules). EXPECTED_SHOT_ASSISTS is the

label: Expected Passes
definition: A model-based estimate of how many passes and set pieces a player is expected to complete successfully, given the difficulty of each attempted pass. The model evaluates contextual factors such as pass distance, angle, opponents, pressure, distance to goal, distance to side lane, phase of play, actio

label: Passes
definition: The change in a team’s goal threat through passes. This includes all progressive passes (increasing the goal threat), regressive passes (passes that reach the teammate but reduce the goal threat) and missed passes (passes intercepted by the opponent, that go out of play or end up offside). These 3 t

label: Ball-in-Play Time
definition: Ball-in-Play Time measures the actual time a player was actively on the pitch during live play - excluding stoppages, substitutions, and inactive phases. It reflects the effective game time that the player was eligible to influence the match, typically calculated by subtracting non-playing phases (e

label: PXT Defend
definition: Influence on the change in one's own goal threat while the team is in the defending phase.

label: Sideway Action
definition: Number of passes that neither take an opponent out of the game nor bring an opponent back into the game.

label: Fouls (Opponent Threat)
definition: The change in the opponent's goal threat due to own fouls. The change is calculated by taking the difference between the opponent's goal threat before the foul and the subsequent opponent's goal threat from an opponent's free kick.

label: Bypassed Opponents
definition: Sum of bypassed opponents. An opponent is bypassed if, after a pass or dribbling, they are further away from their goal than the ball and is therefore no longer able to defend the goal.

label: Expected Goal Assists
definition: A model-based estimate of how many goal assists a player is expected to record from their passes or set pieces. For each pass/set piece, the model calculates the probability that it will become a shot assist leading to a goal (i.e., an assist), based on the quality of the pass and the typical conver

label: DEF_PXT_BALL_WIN
definition: The change in the threat of conceding by winning the ball actively.

label: Successful Passes
definition: A successful pass is any pass that reaches a teammate. In addition, the teammate must have be able to control the ball or the pressure on the teammate must not be too high. E.g. if a striker is given an aerial duel to compete for, this would be counted as a 'neutral ball'.

label: Dribbles (Opponent Threat)
definition: The change in the opponent's goal threat through own dribbling. Progressive dribbling reduces the opponent's goal threat, regressive dribbling and dribbling that ends in a loss of the ball (usually) increases the opponent's goal threat.

label: Fouls
definition: The change in a team’s goal threat due to committing fouls. The change is calculated by taking the difference between a team’s goal threat before a foul is committed and the subsequent goal threat at the opponent's free kick.

label: Ball wins
definition: The change in a team’s goal threat through ball wins from open play. Calculated from the difference between a team’s goal threat after ball win and the threat of the team before the ball win.

label: Failed passes
definition: Sub-KPI of PXT_PASS. The negative change in goal threat due to passing to the opponent or offside. Every pass that lowers the goal threat and ends with the opponent/offside is counted here.

label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates Defenders
definition: Sum of teammate defenders removed from the game. Defender = last 5 players in the given scenario.

label: Opponent shots (Defensive Threat)
definition: The change in the threat of conceding through shots from the opponent. The change in the threat of conceding is distributed equally across all players on the field and we recommend not to assign this value to the players.

label: PXT Attack
definition: Influence on the change in one's own goal threat while the team is in the attacking phase.

label: DEF_PXT_FOUL
definition: The change in the threat of conceding due to a foul.

label: Set pieces (Opponent Threat)
definition: The increase of the goal threat through a ball reception (only positive changes). PXT_REC is not a direct part of the pxT model, because in the pxT model the passer or the set-piece executing player gets the credit for the successful pass/set-piece. We created this KPI anyway so that you can split t

label: Shot xG from Passes
definition: Shot xG from Passes measures the total expected goals (xG) generated from shots that directly followed a player’s passes. The model does not estimate probabilities itself — it simply assigns the xG value of each shot to the pass that created it. All passes that directly lead to a shot are included;

label: Progressive set pieces
definition: Sub-KPI of PXT_SETPIECE. The positive change in one´s goal threat through set-pieces. Every set-piece that increases your goal-goal threat counts here. This also includes set-pieces that end with the opponent if the goal threat is higher after the pass than before the pass.

label: Ball Win Added Teammates Defenders
definition: This is similar to the KPI “Added Teammates", but exclusively relates to the teammate defenders (deepest 5 players from own team) who can still defend their goal. If an opposing striker runs freely towards goal, & a defender wins the ball and brings all 5 previously bypassed defenders back into the

label: Active actions (Defensive Threat)
definition: The change in the threat of conceding by active actions like passes, dribbles, set pieces and blocks.

label: Bypassed Midfielders
definition: Sum of the bypassed midfield players. Midfielders are all outfield players who are not among the last 5 players (defenders) or front 2 players (strikers) in the respective match scenario.

label: Reverse Play Number
definition: Number of passes that bring previously bypassed opponents back into the game.

label: Other
definition: The change in a team's goal threat due to actions that can't be attributed to a player. It consists of 'OPP_PXT_SHOT' and 'OPP_PXT_NOVIDEO'.

label: On-Ball Time
definition: Effective match duration in seconds (including injury time). The time is stopped when the game is interrupted or the ball is out of play.

label: Passive
definition: The change in a team's goal threat while the opponent team is in ball possession. The change of a team's goal threat is calculated based on the responsability model.

label: Neutral Passes
definition: A neutral pass is any pass that is received under high pressure (pressure >= 80). This included passes that end in aerial duels or passes that are followed by a foul.

label: Reverse Play Added Opponents
definition: Sum of opponents who had been previously bypassed and whom the player has brought back into play by the time he completes a pass. Example: If an attacking midfielder receives the ball & dribbles towards the opponent's four-man defence, but then plays a pass to his own centre-back, he brings the 6 pr

label: No Video
definition: The change in a team’s goal threat that you don´t see due to missing video. In addition, changes that cannot be directly assigned to one of your own players or to the generic team count. This includes, for example, the increase in goal threat that comes from having a kick-off. It also includes chang

label: Unsuccessful Passes
definition: An unsuccessful pass is any pass that is intercepted by an opponent or goes out of play or offside. In addition, the opponent intercepting the ball must be able to control the ball or the pressure must not be too high. E.g. if a long ball is played and there is an aerial duel, it would be a "neutral

label: No Video (Opponent Threat)
definition: The change in the opponent's goal threat that you do not see in the video. In addition, changes that cannot be directly assigned to a player or the generic team count. This includes, for example, the increase in goal threat that comes from having a kick-off. It also includes changes due to referee i

label: Goals
definition: Goal

label: DEF PXT Attack
definition: Influence on the change in the opponent's goal threat while the team is in the attacking phase.

label: Critical Ball Loss Number
definition: Number of turnovers in possession in which 4 or more teammates are removed from the game (not goal side of the ball). These ball losses are particularly serious because there are few teammates behind the ball, therefore increasing the risk of conceding a goal.

label: Ball Win Number
definition: Number of possession regains that take at least one opponent player out of the game.

label: Ball Win Removed Opponents
definition: Sum of opponents who, after winning possession, are further away from their own goal than the ball and can therefore no longer defend their goal.

label: Set pieces
definition: The change in a team’s goal threat through set-pieces (free kick, corner, throw-in, goal kick, kick-off). This includes all progressive set-pieces (increasing the goal threat), regressive set-pieces (passes that reach a teammate but reduce goal threat) and set-pieces that end up with possession lost

label: Bypassed Opponents Raw
definition: Bypassed opponents without receiving pressure and without verification by the pass receiver. The analysis takes place between a player's ball reception or set piece and the subsequent ball reception.

label: Dribbles
definition: The change in a team’s goal threat with dribbling. This includes all progressive dribbles (increasing the goal threat), regressive dribbles (dribbles without losing the ball but still reduce the goal threat) and unsuccessful dribbles (dribbles that end up with the ball being lost to the opponent or

label: Bypassed Defenders Raw
definition: Bypassed defenders without receiving pressure and without verification by the pass receiver. The analysis takes place between a player's ball reception or set piece and the subsequent ball reception.

label: No video (Defensive Threat)
definition: The change in the threat of conceding due to an event that is not captured on video.

label: Ball losses (Defensive Threat)
definition: The change in the threat of conceding by losing the ball actively.

label: DEF PXT Defend
definition: Influence on the change in the opponent's goal threat while the team is not in the attacking phase.

label: Ball Loss Number
definition: The number of ball losses due to passes, set-pieces or dribbles.

label: Ball wins (Opponent Threat)
definition: The change in the opponent's goal threat due to own ball conquests. The difference between the opponent's goal threat before the ball is won and the opponent's goal threat after the ball is won.

label: Won Ground Duels Defensive
definition: Won Ground Duels Defensive measures the number of ground duels a player has won in defensive situations, based on the context of the duel (e.g. location, phase of play, team out of possession). Only duels where the player is defending — such as contesting the ball to prevent progression, break up an

label: Won Ground Duels
definition: A ground duel is any action in which at least two players fight for the ball. Here, the players must also have the intention of winning or clearing the ball. Physical contact is a parameter that increases the likelihood that it will be counted as a duel - but it is neither necessary nor sufficient.

label: Bypassed Defenders
definition: Sum of the bypassed defenders. A defender is one of the last 5 outfield players in each given scenario, closest to their own goal. They are bypassed if after a pass or dribbling, they are further away from their goal than the ball and therefore no longer able to defend the goal.

label: Ball Win Added Teammates
definition: Sum of teammates who had been previously bypassed by the opposition but are brought back into the game by a defensive action. If an opposition attacker attempts to make a pass from in between the lines but a defender intercepts this pass, he has added the 6 teammates who had been initially bypassed

label: Ball losses (Opponent Threat)
definition: The change in the opponent's goal threat due to own ball losses. The difference between the opponent's goal threat before losing the ball and the opponent's goal threat after losing the ball.

label: Bypassed Opponents Receiving Number
definition: Number of actions with which opponents are bypassed, from the perspective of the receiver (dribble, pass, cross, set-piece).

label: Won Ground Duels Offensive
definition: Won Ground Duels Offensive measures the number of ground duels a player has won in offensive situations, based on the phase of play, field position, and team possession status. Only duels where the player is attacking - such as attempting to retain or advance possession under pressure - are counted.

label: Regressive passes
definition: Sub-KPI of PXT_PASS. The negative change in a team’s goal threat due to passing to a teammate. Every pass that lowers your goal threat and ends up with a teammate counts here.

label: Ball Loss Added Opponents
definition: Sum of opponents who had been previously bypassed but a player has added back into the game by losing the ball. Example: If an attacking midfielder dribbles towards an opponent's four man defence and then loses the ball and then loses the ball, he will bring the 6 other opponents that had previously

label: Defensive Touches
definition: Any touch on the ball where an opposition player has previously been on the ball is called a defensive touch.

label: Match Share
definition: One match share corresponds to the duration of the match in minutes. (including injury time) E.g. if a match lasts 94 minutes, these 94 minutes correspond to one match share. If another match lasts only 92 minutes, these 92 minutes also represent one match share. If the match proceeds into extra tim

label: PXT_REC
definition: The increase in a team’s goal threat by receiving the ball (only positive changes). PXT_REC is not a direct part of the pxT model, because in the pxT model the passer or the set-piece taking player gets the credit for the successful pass/set-piece. We created this KPI anyway so that you can split th

label: Ball Loss Removed Teammates
definition: A removed teammate is defined as a player who is further away from his own goal than the ball after a loss in possession. If a centre-back plays the ball that is intercepted by the opposing striker who is now free in front of goal, the centre-back has removed 10 of his teammates from the game by los

label: Number of presses
definition: A player applies a 'press' if (1) he is attacking the ball carrying player with the intention to win the ball or (2) he is closer than 1m to the ball carrying player.

label: Bypassed Defenders Without Verification
definition: Bypassed defenders without the pass being 'verified' by the pass receiver.

label: Number of Fouls
definition: Number of Fouls counts how often a player is recorded as having committed a foul during the match, based on tagging or referee decisions.

label: Shot Assists
definition: A shot assist is awarded when a player delivers the final pass or set piece that directly leads to a teammate taking a shot. This includes all types of deliveries such as short or long passes, crosses, through balls, corners, and free kicks, provided the action immediately precedes a shot attempt. T

label: Yellow Card
definition: Yellow cards from your own team.
